key_insight: Chemical order matters: random solid-solution and local chemical order in WTaCrV increase primary defect production under irradiation, yet they help preserve strength and flow stress better than the average-atom model.
engineering_relevance: For radiation-tolerant reactor materials, controlling nanoscale alloy chemistry can be as important as composition itself because it can reduce mechanical degradation even when defect counts rise.
method: Comparison of three configurations: average-atom (AA), random solid-solution (RSS), and local chemical order (LCO)
Newly developed interatomic potentials to isolate chemical effects
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that Frenkel pair production follows NRSS > NLCO > NAA at the same dose, while irradiation strongly degrades AA but has limited impact on the mechanical response of RSS and LCO.
watch_item: The conclusions are based on atomistic simulations with specific potentials, so transferability to experimental reactor conditions and longer timescale damage evolution should be verified.
paper_title: Primary damage and mechanical degradation of WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy: effects of solid-solution and chemical ordering
